paper_title: Ramsey multiplicity and the Turán coloring
summary: No post-2023 paper was found that resolves Conjecture 5.4. The source paper itself proves the weaker Theorem 1.2 (pendant count $t$ exponentially large in $k$ suffices), and the conjecture asks whether any sufficiently large $t$ always works for any $k$-critical base graph. A related 2023 paper (arXiv:2309.06959) extends the Turán-coloring extremality framework to the off-diagonal setting for the same class of graphs, but does not address the threshold question on $t$. The conjecture remains open.
search_queries: Fox Wigderson "two-color bonbon" "k-critical" pendant edges Ramsey multiplicity graph
Ramsey multiplicity bonbon lollipop k-critical pendant edges Fox Wigderson 2024 2025
notes: Web search (3 queries, 3 WebFetch calls) found no paper proving or disproving Conjecture 5.4. The paper arXiv:2309.06959 ('Turán Colourings in Off-Diagonal Ramsey Multiplicity') extends related results to the off-diagonal setting for graphs with pendant edges added to non-3-colorable graphs, but its abstract does not mention the threshold on t that is the subject of Conjecture 5.4. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is now roughly 3 years old and a resolution could have appeared without being prominently indexed.
